text: Well... we did (sorta). Most of my collection comes from when I was young and guns in those days were what you see in the video - taps, butts and imagination. Even when this video was made, Lego guns (that were obviously guns) were just being introduced. The answer to most any question of "Why didn't you use X?", is "We didn't have any X." Why not buy X or complatible lookalikes? We were doing this for fun and we had no real budget! Next time we'll try harder! ;)

text: Most of them are black Taps (faucets) (design 4599, black is 459926).  The agent sometimes has a Round Block 1x1 (cylinder) on the barrel (design 3062, black is 306226).  The agent's gun for the bullet time had the carefully trimmed cylinder part of a Lamp Holder (design 4081, black is 4632571) so the projectile could be stud first.  The rifle dropped by Neo early on is a Flintlock Musket (design 2561).

author: @Demgoht

text:
Can believe the 440 hour total. Check out the other movies of the shoot from other camera angles -  just the bullet dodge bit took 14 hours to shoot. Factor in time to construct all the models (there's a full size Bell helicopter in shot!), and the sourcing of all the bricks, constructing the studio, setting up the equipment, testing etc. - what you're seeing here may be the result of, say, 100 hours shooting, but to set up that hundred hours would easily have taken much longer. 

We salute you!
